title: Local Clusters, Cluster Lifecycle, Conformance, Upgrades, Backup, and Production Def… · Control-Plane Trace: K8s Disaster Recovery Capstone
summary: An operations board must distinguish a local minikube proof from production, upgrade a supported cluster without skipped minors, recover control-plane state, and defend remaining risks. Planned artifact: Add and verify the k8s disaster recovery capstone increment in cluster lifecycle and recovery defense. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Defend control-plane and application backups, etcd snapshots, encryption keys, manifests, external state, restore order, DNS and traffic cutover, recovery objectives, drills, and residual risk.
Plan supported patch and minor upgrades in component order with release notes, deprecated API scans, capacity, drain, disruption, skew, canary, verification, and rollback.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# kubernetes-basics cumulative orchestration portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never invoke kubectl, read kubeconfig, contact a cluster API, or execute host commands.
workspace: kubernetes
version-baseline:
  kubernetes: 1.36.2
  minikube: 1.38.1
  gateway-api: 1.5.1
  helm: 4.2.2
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-cluster
evidence:

steps.title: Predict K8s Disaster Recovery Capstone · Theory K8s Disaster Recovery Capstone
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory board plans a 1.35 to 1.36 control-plane upgrade with skewed kubelets, deprecated APIs, expired recovery assumptions, untested etcd restore, and unclear rollback. Defend compatibility, drain, backup, staged upgrade, recovery, and ownership. Competency probe: Defend control-plane and application backups, etcd snapshots, encryption keys, manifests, external state, restore order, DNS and traffic cutover, recovery objectives, drills, and residual risk.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the K8s Disaster Recovery Capstone contract for the supplied data.

Evidence target: Predict K8s Disaster Recovery Capstone · Theory K8s Disaster Recovery Capstone.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for defend control-plane and application backups, etcd snapshots, encryption keys, manifests, external state, restore order, dns and traffic cutover, recovery objectives, drills, and residual risk. For Predict K8s Disaster Recovery Capstone · Theory K8s Disaster Recovery Capstone, keep K8s Disaster Recovery Capstone connected to mechanically reviewable Kubernetes object, status, and transfer evidence.

Defend control-plane and application backups, etcd snapshots, encryption keys, manifests, external state, restore order, DNS and traffic cutover, recovery objectives, drills, and residual risk. Apply it to the concrete input and state the observable result.

An etcd snapshot alone restores every application volume, external dependency, credential, and user-visible service. That claim does not survive the predict k8s disaster recovery capstone evidence boundary.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Name context, namespace, API identity, desired spec, observed status, controller authority, workload, network, storage, identity, policy, and failure-domain boundaries; predict reconciliation; review the smallest deterministic manifest change; then verify a failure, repair, rollback, and disposable-cluster transfer limit.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example board plans a 1.35 to 1.36 control-plane upgrade with skewed kubelets, deprecated APIs, expired recovery assumptions, untested etcd restore, and unclear rollback. Defend compatibility, drain, backup, staged upgrade, recovery, and ownership. Competency probe: Defend control-plane and application backups, etcd snapshots, encryption keys, manifests, external state, restore order, DNS and traffic cutover, recovery objectives, drills, and residual risk. A defensible solution applies K8s Disaster Recovery Capstone, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to cluster lifecycle and recovery defense.

content.code:
# Evidence: k8s-worked-k8s-cluster-lifecycle-upgrade-recovery-2
  - competency: k8s-worked-k8s-cluster-lifecycle-upgrade-recovery-2
    artifact: cluster-recovery-defense.yaml
    decision: worked k8s cluster lifecycle upgrade recovery 2
    environment: rootless development cluster with an empty namespace
    fault-injection: make an admission dependency unavailable during an upgrade
    retained-proof: accessible incident timeline with causal regression evidence
    case-key: worked2
    change: |
      baseline: kubernetes-1.36.2
      local-proof: minikube-1.38.1
      distribution: supported-and-conformant
      upgrade: patch-current-then-next-minor
      order: api-server-then-controllers-then-kubelet
      drain: capacity-and-pdb-verified
      etcd: encrypted-snapshot-restored
      application-state: independently-restored
      rto-rpo: rehearsed
    verify: kubectl get nodes -o json && kubeadm upgrade plan && etcdctl snapshot status backup.db
    failure: Reject minikube as production proof, skipped minor upgrades, kubelet-first order, drain without capacity, etcd-only recovery, or backup without restore.
    repair: Confirm support and skew, scan removed APIs, canary components and workloads, preserve keys and state, rehearse restore order, and verify user service.
content.caption: Runnable Kubernetes object and transfer-evidence contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: An etcd snapshot alone restores every application volume, external dependency, credential, and user-visible service. This fails the K8s Disaster Recovery Capstone contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

content.code:
# Evidence: k8s-disaster-recovery-capstone-1d7wfa
  - competency: k8s-disaster-recovery-capstone
    artifact: cluster-recovery-defense.yaml
    decision: disaster recovery capstone
    environment: high-latency edge cluster with intermittent control-plane access
    fault-injection: rotate a projected token while the API dependency slows
    retained-proof: scheduler event eligible domains skew and placement result
    case-key: 1d7wfa
    change: |
      baseline: kubernetes-1.36.2
      local-proof: minikube-1.38.1
      distribution: supported-and-conformant
      upgrade: patch-current-then-next-minor
      order: api-server-then-controllers-then-kubelet
      drain: capacity-and-pdb-verified
      etcd: encrypted-snapshot-restored
      application-state: independently-restored
      rto-rpo: rehearsed
    verify: kubectl get nodes -o json && kubeadm upgrade plan && etcdctl snapshot status backup.db
    failure: Reject minikube as production proof, skipped minor upgrades, kubelet-first order, drain without capacity, etcd-only recovery, or backup without restore.
    repair: Confirm support and skew, scan removed APIs, canary components and workloads, preserve keys and state, rehearse restore order, and verify user service.
